volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section 2.4.705 - Section 2.4.751b)

sections.label: Section 2.4.705
sections.original: artibus tribus trís demeritas dem laetitias , de tribus
sections.translation: obtained over three persons through craftiness and through subtlety , which in this very little sealed packet I have brought unto you [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.4.707
sections.translation: That ’ s the fellow ;

sections.label: Section 2.4.707b
sections.translation: how the hang-dog does bluster just like a tragedian .

sections.label: Section 2.4.707c
sections.translation: Advance a step on thy side towards me . Boldly stretch forth thine arm for greeting .

sections.label: Section 2.4.709
sections.original: Dic utrum Spemne an Salutem te salutem , Pseudole ?
sections.translation: Tell me , Pseudolus , whether , as Hope or as Safety , must I greet you ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.710
sections.translation: Why both .

sections.label: Section 2.4.710b
sections.translation: As both I greet you . But what has been done ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.710c
sections.translation: Why are you silent ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.711
sections.translation: I have carried this person here .

sections.label: Section 2.4.711b
sections.translation: How ? Carried him here ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.711c
sections.translation: Brought , I meant to say .

sections.label: Section 2.4.712
sections.translation: Who ’ s this person ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.712b
sections.translation: Charinus .

sections.label: Section 2.4.712c
sections.translation: Well done ; I return him thanks [*] then .

sections.label: Section 2.4.713
sections.original: Quin tu si quid opust , mi audacter imperas ?
sections.translation: Will you then boldly enjoin me what it is necessary to do ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.713b
sections.translation: My thanks , so far . May it be well with you , Charinus ; I don ’ t like that we should be troublesome to you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.715
sections.original: Vos molestos mihi ? molestumst mi id quidem .
sections.translation: You , troublesome to me ? Now , really , that ’ s troublesome .

sections.label: Section 2.4.715b
sections.translation: Well , then , wait a moment .

sections.label: Section 2.4.716
sections.translation: What ’ s that ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.716b
sections.original: Epistulam modo hanc íntercepi et sumbolum .
sections.translation: This letter have I just now intercepted , and the token .

sections.label: Section 2.4.717
sections.translation: Token ? What token ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.717b
sections.translation: The one that was brought just now from the Captain . His servant , who was bringing it , with five mineae of silver , who came to fetch your mistress hence , him I have just now bamboozled .

sections.label: Section 2.4.720
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.720b
sections.original: Horum causa haec agitur spectatorum fabula :
sections.translation: For the sake of these Spectators the play is being performed ; they know , who were present here ; you I ’ ll tell at another time .

sections.label: Section 2.4.722
sections.translation: What are we to do then ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.722b
sections.translation: You shall this day embrace your mistress at liberty .

sections.label: Section 2.4.723
sections.translation: What , I ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.723b
sections.original: Tu istic ipsus , inquam , si quidem hoc vivet caput ;
sections.translation: Your own self , I say , if indeed this head shall exist so long ; if you ’ ll only quickly find me out a man .

sections.label: Section 2.4.724b
sections.translation: Of what description ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.724c
sections.translation: A cunning ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.725
sections.original: callidum , doctum , qui quando principium prehenderit ,
sections.translation: crafty and clever one , who , when he has once taken hold of the beginning , may by his own ingenuity still hold fast upon what it behoves him to do ; one , too , who has not often been seen here .

sections.label: Section 2.4.727b
sections.translation: If he is a slave , does that matter at all ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.728b
sections.translation: Why , I ’ d much rather have him than a free man .

sections.label: Section 2.4.729
sections.original: Posse opinor me dare hominem tibi malum et doctum domo ,
sections.translation: I think that I ’ m able to procure for you a cunning and clever fellow ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.730
sections.original: qui a patre advenit Carysto nec dum exiit ex aedibus
sections.translation: that has lately come to my father from Carystus [*] , and hasn ’ t as yet gone anywhere out the house , and who never visited Athens before yesterday .

sections.label: Section 2.4.732
sections.original: Bene iuvas . sed quinque inventis opus est argenti minis
sections.translation: You assist in right earnest . But I have need to borrow five minae of silver , which I shall repay this day , for his father ( pointing to CALIDORUS ) owes it me .

sections.label: Section 2.4.734
sections.translation: I ’ ll lend it ; don ’ t seek it anywhere else .

sections.label: Section 2.4.734b
sections.translation: O , how convenient a person for me .

sections.label: Section 2.4.735
sections.original: etiam opust chlamyde et machaera et petaso .
sections.translation: I have need of a scarf as well , a sword , and a broad-brimmed hat .

sections.label: Section 2.4.735b
sections.translation: I can provide them from my house .

sections.label: Section 2.4.736
sections.original: Di immortales , non Charinus míhi hic quidem , sed Copiast .
sections.translation: Immortal Gods ! surely this is not Charinus for me , but Abundance . But this servant , who is come here from Carystus , is there anything in him ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.738
sections.translation: Plenty of the stinking goat [*] in him .

sections.label: Section 2.4.738b
sections.translation: It befits the fellow , then , to have a tunic with long sleeves [*] Has the chap anything sharp [*] in his breast ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.739b
sections.translation: Aye , of the very sharpest .

sections.label: Section 2.4.740
sections.original: Quid , si opus sít ut dulce promat indidem , ecquid habet ?
sections.translation: But if it is necessary for him to draw forth what is sweet from the same place , has he aught of that ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.740b
sections.translation: Do you ask that He has wine of myrrh , sweet raisin wine , spice wine [*] honey wine , sweets of every sort . Why , he once began to set up a hot liquor-shop in his breast .

sections.label: Section 2.4.743
sections.original: Eugepae , lepide , Charine , meo me ludo lamberas .
sections.translation: Bravissimo ! Why , Charinus , you beat me cleverly [*] at my own game . But what am I to say is the name of this servant ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.744b
sections.translation: Simmia .

sections.label: Section 2.4.745
sections.translation: In a difficulty , does he understand how to twirl about ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.745b
sections.translation: A whirlwind [*] is not so ready as he .

sections.label: Section 2.4.746
sections.translation: Is he shrewd at all ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.746b
sections.translation: In mischievous tricks [*] very often .

sections.label: Section 2.4.747
sections.translation: How , when he ’ s caught in the fact ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.747b
sections.translation: He ’ s a very eel ; he slips out .

sections.label: Section 2.4.748
sections.translation: Is this fellow an experienced one ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.748b
sections.translation: A public ordinance [*] is not more experienced .

sections.label: Section 2.4.749
sections.original: Probus homo est , ut praedicare te audio .
sections.translation: He is a suitable person , according to what I hear you say .

sections.label: Section 2.4.749b
sections.translation: Aye , and from this you may know it .

sections.label: Section 2.4.750
sections.original: ubi te aspexerit , narrabit ultro quid sese velis .
sections.translation: When he looks at you he ’ ll tell , of his own accord , what it is you want with him . But what are you about to do ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.751b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you . When I ’ ve dressed up my man , I intend to make him become the pretended servant of the Captain ; let him take this token to the procurer , with five minae of silver . There ’ s the whole plot for you .
